author: azyarashi
description: Render math expressions with Typst instead of MathJax.

manifest.author: azyarashi
manifest.description: Render math expressions with Typst instead of MathJax.
manifest.name: Typst Mate

name: Typst Mate

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - It is now correctly syntax-highlighted. - SymbolSuggest is now shown only when in math mode. - The .typ Editor has been greatly enhanced. - In the processor, the `Fit to parent width` option has been abolished, and `Fit to note width` has been introduced. Set the value `WIDTH` to the page. - In the processor, the `Use replace all` option has been introduced. - `Apply processor to MathJax` has been added to Advanced Settings. When this is enabled, `{CODE}` will be replaced even in a processor where all MathJax engines are enabled.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - It is now correctly syntax-highlighted. - SymbolSuggest is now shown only when in math mode. - The .typ Editor has been greatly enhanced. - In the processor, the `Fit to parent width` option has been abolished, and `Fit to note width` has been introduced. Set the value `WIDTH` to the page. - In the processor, the `Use replace all` option has been introduced. - `Apply processor to MathJax` has been added to Advanced Settings. When this is enabled, `{CODE}` will be replaced even in a processor where all MathJax engines are enabled.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ## Important Starting with this version, inline math expects **only inline elements**. Therefore, please update your inline-math processor as follows: ```typst #set page(margin: (y: 0.3125em)) ${CODE}$ // display style // #math.equation($ {CODE} $, block: false) ``` The previous approach: ``` $ inline(zws {CODE}) $ // workaround for obsidian ``` will make the appearance look incorrect. Also, I haven't been able to prepare the documentation yet, so I am reposting the contents of 2.2.28 below for now. ---- 2 new awesome features by @Batres3 and several fixes. - **The ability to define custom variables for a file** The `definitions` property (list) in the note's frontmatter is now automatically added to equations as let-rules. For example, if you define `fib(n) = if n <= 2 { 1 } else { fib(n - 1) + fib(n - 2) }`, it can be called anywhere within the file. - **The ability to import .typ files based on the tags of the file** A feature to automatically import Typst files according to the tags in the note. The base path can be configured via the "Import Path" setting (found in the Advanced tab, defaults to `.typst`). For example, if a tag such as `#a/b` exists, `#import "tags/a.b.typ": *` will be automatically added to equations. For details, please refer to the PR below: #30 ## Other Features - Although a .typ editor has not been implemented yet, .typ files can now be created from the context menu to support the features above. - By specifying `mathjax` in the `math-engine` property (text) of the frontmatter, Typst will be disabled for that entire file.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ## Important Starting with this version, inline math expects **only inline elements**. Therefore, please update your inline-math processor as follows: ```typst #set page(margin: (y: 0.3125em)) ${CODE}$ // display style // #math.equation($ {CODE} $, block: false) ``` The previous approach: ``` $ inline(zws {CODE}) $ // workaround for obsidian ``` will make the appearance look incorrect. Also, I haven't been able to prepare the documentation yet, so I am reposting the contents of 2.2.28 below for now. ---- 2 new awesome features by @Batres3 and several fixes. - **The ability to define custom variables for a file** The `definitions` property (list) in the note's frontmatter is now automatically added to equations as let-rules. For example, if you define `fib(n) = if n <= 2 { 1 } else { fib(n - 1) + fib(n - 2) }`, it can be called anywhere within the file. - **The ability to import .typ files based on the tags of the file** A feature to automatically import Typst files according to the tags in the note. The base path can be configured via the "Import Path" setting (found in the Advanced tab, defaults to `.typst`). For example, if a tag such as `#a/b` exists, `#import "tags/a.b.typ": *` will be automatically added to equations. For details, please refer to the PR below: #30 ## Other Features - Although a .typ editor has not been implemented yet, .typ files can now be created from the context menu to support the features above. - By specifying `mathjax` in the `math-engine` property (text) of the frontmatter, Typst will be disabled for that entire file.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: This update only contains fixes, so the following is 2.2.28. ---- 2 new awesome features by @Batres3 and several fixes. - **The ability to define custom variables for a file** The `definitions` property (list) in the note's frontmatter is now automatically added to equations as let-rules. For example, if you define `fib(n) = if n <= 2 { 1 } else { fib(n - 1) + fib(n - 2) }`, it can be called anywhere within the file. - **The ability to import .typ files based on the tags of the file** A feature to automatically import Typst files according to the tags in the note. The base path can be configured via the "Import Path" setting (found in the Advanced tab, defaults to `.typst`). For example, if a tag such as `#a/b` exists, `#import "tags/a.b.typ": *` will be automatically added to equations. For details, please refer to the PR below: #30 ## Other Features - Although a .typ editor has not been implemented yet, .typ files can now be created from the context menu to support the features above. - By specifying `mathjax` in the `math-engine` property (text) of the frontmatter, Typst will be disabled for that entire file. ## Important (Regarding Styles) In Obsidian, the processor must be configured as follows to render inline math correctly. Please update your settings individually. please select inline-middle style and ```typst $ inline(zws {CODE}) $ ``` ## Minor Changes The following settings have been added to Style-Settings: - **Match MathJax Spacing** Sets a 1em margin for display math and code blocks, similar to MathJax. This is effective in Reading Mode.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: 2 new awesome features by @Batres3 and several fixes. - **The ability to define custom variables for a file** The `definitions` property (list) in the note's frontmatter is now automatically added to equations as let-rules. For example, if you define `fib(n) = if n <= 2 { 1 } else { fib(n - 1) + fib(n - 2) }`, it can be called anywhere within the file. - **The ability to import .typ files based on the tags of the file** A feature to automatically import Typst files according to the tags in the note. The base path can be configured via the "Import Path" setting (found in the Advanced tab, defaults to `.typst`). For example, if a tag such as `#a/b` exists, `#import "tags/a.b.typ": *` will be automatically added to equations. For details, please refer to the PR below: #30 ## Other Features - Although a .typ editor has not been implemented yet, .typ files can now be created from the context menu to support the features above. - By specifying `mathjax` in the `math-engine` property (text) of the frontmatter, Typst will be disabled for that entire file. ## Important (Regarding Styles) In Obsidian, the processor must be configured as follows to render inline math correctly. Please update your settings individually. please select inline-middle style and ```typst $ inline(zws {CODE}) $ ``` ## Minor Changes The following settings have been added to Style-Settings: - **Match MathJax Spacing** Sets a 1em margin for display math and code blocks, similar to MathJax. This is effective in Reading Mode.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Thanks to @BridgeSenseDev, memory explosion no longer occurs when downloading WASM on Android devices

---

During version `2.2.x`, starting with this release `2.2.0` I will only publish minor releases that use **the latest Typst 0.14.x**.
If you need **Typst 0.13.1**, please downgrade to `2.1.7` using the BRAT plugin.

In Typst `0.14.0` or after, the following line in the default preamble:

```typst
#let scr(it) = text(features: ("ss01",), box($cal(it)$))
```

is no longer necessary, please remove it.

**Due to university entrance exams, I won't be able to make any major changes for about two months!**

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
small fixes (ui improvements and fix default preamble)
The following is the content of 2.2.22

New Features:
- add conceal symbol (try to disable `complementSymbolWithUnicode`!)
- add TyLax to Typst Tools, enabling bidirectional conversion between:
  - Typst Markup <-> LaTeX Document
  - Typst Math (`typm`) <-> LaTeX Equation
  - **CeTZ <-> TikZ**!

Changes:
- **arrow Right keys can no longer be used for completion. Please use Tab instead.**

Improvements:
- enhance shortcut handling: shortcuts are now triggered by a 0.25-second long press instead of immediate execution.
- improve code block preview behavior

A tiny toast to Typst's 50k stars🤩

**Due to university entrance exams, I won't be able to make any major changes for about two months!**

Happy New Year!

----

During version `2.2.x`, starting with this release `2.2.0` I will only publish minor releases that use **the latest Typst 0.14.x**.
If you need **Typst 0.13.1**, please downgrade to `2.1.7` using the BRAT plugin.

In Typst `0.14.0` or after, the following line in the default preamble:

```typst
#let scr(it) = text(features: ("ss01",), box($cal(it)$))
```

is no longer necessary, please remove it.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
After the update the behavior may become unstable. If that happens, you will only need to restart Obsidian once.

- add an option `Revert Tab to Default`
- change `smallcaps` font setting to reference the default font (this differs from Typst's default behavior but makes smallcaps easy to use in math mode.)
- add linter display
- add context menu (right-click on desktop app, long-press on mobile app)
  - Copy as SVG (Obsidian Theme)
  - Copy as SVG (Typst Default)
  - Copy as PNG (Transparent)
  - Copy as PNG (Opaque)
  - Copy as img tag (for embedding into Obsidian notes)

----

During version `2.2.x`, starting with this release `2.2.0` I will only publish minor releases that use **the latest Typst 0.14.x**.
If you need **Typst 0.13.1**, please downgrade to `2.1.7` using the BRAT plugin.

In Typst `0.14.0` or after, the following line in the default preamble:

```typst
#let scr(it) = text(features: ("ss01",), box($cal(it)$))
```

is no longer necessary, please remove it.
